rule_text: Setup: use every cell of an empty 3×3 board. Player 0 moves first; turns alternate, and each move places one stone on an empty cell. Player 0's first stone must be on the top row; Player 1's first stone must be on the left column. After that player's first stone, a placement is legal only if it is orthogonally adjacent to a friendly component and it is adjacent to at least two distinct friendly components under that relation. A player wins when one friendly orthogonally connected group contains at least 3 stones. If the next player has no legal placement and the previous move did not win, the previous player wins.
